_note: Injection rail (Hatter-side evidence boundary). Scores UNTRUSTED external evidence from provider snippet fields in the source registry with Llama Prompt Guard 2 86M for prompt injection. Registry metadata, titles, URLs, retrieval timestamps, provider names, and agent-generated query strings are not scored. The agent-authored synthesis doc is NOT scanned (benign security prose). A mesh operator may override this file at .caterpillar/guardrails/oracle/injection.json; the workflow prefers that path when present. config_sha256 in the rail report pins whichever config ran.
_model_revision_note: PINNED to the HF COMMIT SHA resolved at cert run 26732315667 (2026-06-01, meta-llama/Llama-Prompt-Guard-2-86M main → a8ded8e6…). Replay retrieves the EXACT same weights and compares this value, so a moved tag surfaces as rail-replay-mismatch. To bump the model: read the new resolved_revision from a cert run + update this SHA in the same change. A missing pin now fails closed (require_pinned_revision:true).
_require_pinned_note: true → inject_check.py refuses to run on an unpinned tag (replay determinism + no hard-gating on drifting weights). false → advisory-only. PROMOTED to a required hard gate 2026-06-01 after cert (5b) passed green (hostile exit=2 blocked, benign exit=0).
_block_on_needs_review_note: false means the review band is recorded + annotated but does not block a PR; only score >= block_threshold fails. This keeps ambiguous model signals honest while fixtures prove direct injection still exits 2.
_benign_context_note: A unit scoring >= block_threshold is demoted to needs_review (never hard-blocked) when its surrounding window is QUOTED / CITED / CODE-FENCED — the corpus discussing an example attack, not delivering one. This is the injection analogue of the PII rail's benign-identifier carve-out and is what keeps a security-research corpus from hard-failing wholesale. Tune against the fixture corpus; never blind.
